purpose: House of Quality (QFD) — maps Performance Criteria (PCs, the voice of the customer) to Engineering Characteristics (ECs, the language of the designer) via a relationship matrix, with roof (EC-EC interactions), back porch (competitive scoring), and basement (measurement units, targets, imputed importance, difficulty, cost).

legend.purpose: Visual key printed at rows 10-15. These are display symbols — internal cell values must be the numeric mapping below for formulas to work.

second_floor.purpose: Engineering Characteristics — the measurable design variables. Up to 26.

ec_id_column.note: Pre-populated auto-increment (D3=1, D4=D3+1, …). Do not overwrite.

second_floor.ec_id_header: E29:AD29 hold EC numbers 1..26 via formula (E29=1, F29=E29+1, …). Do not overwrite.

ec_name_cells.pattern: EC_n name goes in the diagonal cell. Per cell-map convention: row = n+2, column index = n+4 (col E for n=1, AD for n=26).
ec_name_cells.note: Template is blank here; consult the QFD Sample sheet for the exact diagonal visual convention before writing.

ec_direction_of_change.row: 32 (or the row below the last EC header name)
ec_direction_of_change.cells_per_ec: One cell in column E..AD at the direction-of-change row.

front_porch.purpose: Performance Criteria — what the customer cares about. Up to 12.

columns.name: full_attribute
columns.note: The full customer-language statement of the performance criterion.

columns.name: short_name
columns.note: Terse label used in diagrams / narrative.

columns.name: relative_importance
columns.constraint: All D33:D44 weights must sum to 1.00 (±0.01). D32 has SUM formula — verify it shows 1.00.

direction_of_change_column.note: PC-level direction of change. Some templates place it here at row 31; in this template C33:C44 also serves as the short-name column. Confirm by visual inspection before writing.

main_floor.purpose: Relationship Matrix — for each (PC row, EC column) pair, the strength/sign of the relationship.
main_floor.blank_policy: 0 or empty = no relationship. Empty cells are treated as 0 by the imputed-importance SUMPRODUCT.

asymmetric_values.format: string like '-1/+1' — used when the EC's direction of change flips the sign of the relationship (e.g., smaller is better at low end, larger is better at high end).
asymmetric_values.warning: String values break the SUMPRODUCT in basement row 50. Either switch to signed numeric when possible, or compute imputed importance manually.

roof.purpose: EC × EC interrelationships — captures how pairs of engineering characteristics affect each other.
roof.cell_formula: For EC_i × EC_j where i < j: row = j + 2, column index = i + 4. Example: EC1 ↔ EC2 at E4. EC7 ↔ EC26 at K28.
roof.critical_rule: Write values in the LOWER triangle only (below-left of the diagonal). NOT the upper triangle.

back_porch.purpose: Competitive scoring — how each option (your design at low/high/target + competitors B, C) scores against each Performance Criterion.

columns.name: option_a_target
columns.note: In the template only AE, AF, AH, AI are pre-seeded with 0; AG (target) is not. Write values here for your target design.

unweighted.value_scale: 1–5 customer perception (1=poor, 5=excellent). Some templates use 0–5 with 0 meaning 'not applicable'.

weighted.note: Pre-populated formulas: each weighted cell = unweighted × PC importance weight. Do not overwrite. AL (weighted A-target) is also not in the seeded formulas — inspect before assuming.

basement.purpose: Per-EC operational data and derived metrics. One cell per EC (E..AD).

measurement_units.label: Measurement Units
measurement_units.note: Units for each EC (e.g., 'ms', '$', 'N/A', 'Table U1'). Qualitative ECs reference a lookup table.

external_thresholds.label: (External) Requirement Thresholds
external_thresholds.note: Hard min/max constraints per EC (e.g., '≤100 ms', '≥99.9%'). Options violating these are rejected regardless of score.

targets.label: Targets
targets.note: Your design target value per EC — the deliverable of the QFD exercise.

technical_difficulty.label: Technical Difficulty (1 Low, 5 High)
technical_difficulty.note: Engineering team estimate after seeing imputed importance ranks. Phase 08 re-evaluation.

write_protocol.rationale: TEMPLATE_CELL-MAP.md warns that openpyxl destroys merged cells, borders, and roof formatting when saving. AppleScript (macOS) preserves all template formatting.

openpyxl.use: Safe for inspection and validation (load_workbook + iter_rows). Never save() back to the template.

write_protocol.applescript_sketch: See TEMPLATE_CELL-MAP.md §'AppleScript Automation (macOS)' for the safe-write protocol (backup → quit Excel → open → write cells → save).

validation_rules: Every PC row filled must have full_attribute + short_name + relative_importance.
Every EC column filled must have a name (diagonal cell) + direction_of_change + measurement_unit + target.
Roof values live ONLY in the lower triangle (row ≥ column_index - 1).
Relationship matrix values are numeric in [-2, -1, 0, 1, 2] OR asymmetric strings — asymmetric breaks row-50 SUMPRODUCT.
Back porch unweighted scores are in the scale used in the course (typically 1–5).
Competitor B/C names in C46 and C47 replace the generic 'Competitor Name B/C' labels.
known_issues: Cell-map §'Second Floor' lists EC diagonal positions as (row n+2, col n+5); EC columns as (col n+4). The n+5 vs n+4 offset should be confirmed against the QFD Sample sheet before programmatic diagonal writes.
Row 32 shows C32='Short Name' while C31='Direction of Change ↑ ↓'. The Direction-of-Change column placement differs across QFD variants; confirm the intended cell before writing.
AG (A-target) back-porch column has no pre-seeded formula in AL (its weighted counterpart). Verify AL formula exists, or write it manually.
